problem_addressed: Missing instances in Earth Observation time series data due to satellite failure or cloud occlusion introduce uncertainties that degrade regression model performance.
core_claim: Monte Carlo Concrete Temporal Dropout (MC-ConcTD) improves predictive performance and uncertainty calibration compared to existing approaches by learning optimal dropout distributions.
key_contributions: Introduction of Monte Carlo Temporal Dropout (MC-TD) to simulate missing data during inference
Development of Monte Carlo Concrete Temporal Dropout (MC-ConcTD) that learns optimal dropout distributions
Demonstration of superior performance across three Earth Observation datasets
novelty: Unlike previous approaches that use fixed dropout ratios or data augmentation, MC-ConcTD learns the optimal dropout distribution as a learnable parameter, eliminating the need for costly manual tuning.
method_summary: The method applies dropout at the input level during inference, using either fixed or learnable dropout ratios to simulate missing time-steps. Monte Carlo sampling with 20 samples is used for uncertainty quantification.
method_components: Temporal Dropout that masks input data using Bernoulli distribution
Concrete distribution for continuous relaxation of dropout with learnable parameter
Monte Carlo sampling for uncertainty quantification
key_innovation: The use of learnable dropout parameters (α) in the Concrete distribution, which eliminates the need for manual tuning of dropout ratios while improving uncertainty calibration.
assumptions: Input uncertainty propagates through the model to result in epistemic uncertainty
Dropout approximates Bayesian inference in neural networks
The learnable dropout parameter will converge to an optimal value
theoretical_grounding: The method is theoretically grounded in the relationship between input and output uncertainty (Valdenegro-Toro et al. [49]) and the Bayesian approximation properties of dropout (Gal et al. [13]). The Concrete distribution provides a continuous relaxation of the discrete dropout operation.
headline_result: MC-ConcTD achieves best overall results with R2 of 0.79 on SwissYield dataset
ablations: MC-TD vs MC-ConcTD comparison showing MC-ConcTD's adaptability in learning different dropout values
improvement_over_baseline: MC-ConcTD shows R2 improvement of 0.01 on SwissYield, 0.03 on PM2.5, and equal performance on LFMC compared to MC-Dropout
best_result: MC-ConcTD achieves best overall results with R2 of 0.79 on SwissYield dataset
title: An Analysis of Temporal Dropout in Earth Observation Time Series for Regression Tasks
